perturbation.original_text: FAU has a legal responsibility to safeguard the confidentiality and security of our patients’ protected health information (“PHI”) as well as operational, proprietary, and st udent and employee information (collectively “FAU Confidential Information”). Thi s information may include, but i s not limited to, patient health records, as well as information regarding human res ources, payroll, fiscal matters, research, and strategic pl anning, and may exist in any form, including electronic, video, spoken, or written.
perturbation.changed_text: FAU has a responsibility to safeguard the confidentiality and security of our patients’ protected health information (“PHI”) as well as operational, proprietary, and st udent and employee information (collectively “FAU Confidential Information”). Thi s information may include, but i s not limited to, patient health records, as well as information regarding human res ources, payroll, fiscal matters, research, and strategic pl anning, and may exist in any form, including electronic, video, spoken, or written.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'legal', this introduces an in-text contradiction by weakening the obligation to safeguard PHI. It creates uncertainty, potentially conflicting with the later statement that violations may lead to legal recourse. This creates uncertainty in enforcement and accountability.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Paragraph under the last bullet point starting with: 'I understand that violations'
perturbation.contradicted_text: I understand that violations of this Agreement may result in re vocation of my user privileges and/or disciplinary action, up to and including termination of my employment, affiliation or association with FAU, and that FAU may seek any civil or crimin al recourse and/or equitable relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text states FAU 'has a legal responsibility' to safeguard information. The changed text removes the word 'legal', weakening the obligation. The contradicted text states that violations of the agreement may result in civil or criminal recourse. By removing 'legal', the modified text implies that there is no longer a legal obligation to safeguard the information, which contradicts the possibility of 'criminal recourse' for violations, making the obligation weaker and creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I understand that access to health information created, receive d, or maintained by FAU or its affiliates is limited to those who have a valid business, medic al, or professional need to know the information. I understand that FAU has implemented administrati ve, technical, and physical safeguards to protect the confi dentiality and security of PHI, and I agree not to bypass or disable these safeguards.
perturbation.changed_text: I understand that access to health information created, receive d, or maintained by FAU or its affiliates is limited to those who have a valid business, medic al, or professional need to know the information. I understand that FAU has implemented administrati ve, technical, and physical safeguards to protect the confi dentiality and security of PHI, and I agree to bypass these safeguards only with explicit permission from my supervisor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says 'I agree not to bypass or disable these safeguards'. Now, the text was changed to introduce an in-text contradiction related to bypassing or disabling security safeguards. It changes the user's commitment from never bypassing safeguards to bypassing them with supervisor approval. This contradicts the initial purpose of having safeguards, creates risks and ambiguity in enforcement.
perturbation.location: Paragraph under bullet point starting with: 'I understand that access'
perturbation.contradicted_location: Paragraph under bullet point starting with: 'I understand that access'
perturbation.contradicted_text: I understand that access to health information created, receive d, or maintained by FAU or its affiliates is limited to those who have a valid business, medic al, or professional need to know the information. I understand that FAU has implemented administrati ve, technical, and physical safeguards to protect the confi dentiality and security of PHI, and I agree to bypass these safeguards only with explicit permission from my supervisor.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'I agree not to bypass or disable these safeguards.' The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction by changing this commitment to bypassing them with supervisor approval. This directly contradicts the initial commitment and creates a conflict with the purpose of safeguards. Since the original text specified no bypass and the changed text allows bypass with permission, and the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, the contradiction is clear and impactful. This adheres to the 'Omissions' - In Text Contradiction definition since essential language was altered.
